articles.title: Human Rights: Amnesty International warns of escalating ethnic cleansing as Israel accelerates West Bank annexations
articles.summary: Amnesty International has accused Israel of pursuing a state-led campaign of annexation and ethnic cleansing in the occupied West Bank, warning that the forced displacement of Palestinian communities has intensified sharply since 2023. The organisation’s latest report argues that international inaction is enabling violations of international law and calls for urgent measures to halt further dispossession and displacement.

articles.title: ‘EL TERRORISTA’: ‘They thought we were terrorists’ — Mexican sports journalists home after SA drone arrests
articles.summary: Two Mexican sports journalists who were arrested in Gauteng, allegedly because they flew a drone over a Jewish college, have pleaded guilty, paid a fine, and returned home. They say they were considered terrorists and kept behind bars for a week.

articles.title: XENOPHOBIC VIOLENCE: Nigeria threatens retaliation against SA for attacks on its citizens

articles.title: WHAT NEXT: Here’s how to take Kirstenbosch to greatness
articles.summary: This series on the gardens has had, within 48 hours, nearly 80,000 readers, and provoked an unprecedented number of written comments. It’s an indication of both deep love and concern for Kirstenbosch and the way ahead.In researching these stories, we received many suggestions about what a range of people – specialists to tourists – think should be done to make Kirstenbosch better. We decided, in view of that interest, to publish this list in the hope that it may be of use. Some points are a bit ...

articles.title: LOCAL ELECTIONS 2026: ‘No one is above the law’ — DA Ekurhuleni candidate outlines five key promises
articles.summary: The DA’s Ekurhuleni mayoral candidate, Khathu Rasilingwane, has pledged to root out corruption, improve service delivery and restore accountability as the party intensifies its campaign to secure outright control of the metro in November’s local government elections.

articles.title: IDENTITY CRISIS: UCT Children’s Institute and Home Affairs clash in court over late birth registration backlog
articles.summary: In a pivotal court case, the UCT Children’s Institute challenges Home Affairs over delays in late birth registrations, asserting that undocumented children face severe rights violations.

articles.title: MADLANGA COMMISSION: Witness I accused of cover-up in attempted R286m drug theft
articles.summary: Commissioners and evidence leaders at the Madlanga Commission challenged Witness I’s claim that he went to the scene of a R286m cocaine bust because it related to a previous investigation. They questioned whether he was there to legitimise an attempt by law enforcement officials to steal the drugs.

articles.title: WEATHER WATCH: It’s here! Japanese Meteorological Agency says El Niño has emerged
articles.summary: Global forecasters have been warning for months that this brewing El Niño could be on steroids – with some commentators likening it to a ‘Godzilla event’ – which, given its historical spoor, would have massive social and economic consequences in southern Africa.

articles.title: SCOPA: Joburg leaders grilled for failing to question JDA boss arrested with R321,00 cash
articles.summary: Johannesburg’s top leaders faced tough questions in Parliament on Tuesday over their response to the arrest of Johannesburg Development Agency CEO Themba Mathibe, after R321,000 in cash was found at his home during a corruption investigation.

articles.title: GROUNDUP: Over 1,000 migrants flee Durban settlement after being ordered to leave
articles.summary: Immigrants, mostly from Malawi, chased from Burnwood have spent days camped in a field.

articles.title: THE CONVERSATION: What is lipoprotein(a) cholesterol, or Lp(a)? And can you lower yours?
articles.summary: Most people know about “good” and “bad” cholesterol. But few realise there is another type called lipoprotein(a). It can raise the risk of heart attacks and strokes, even in people who do everything right.

articles.title: SEXUAL OFFENDERS: Vetting crisis: Efforts hampered by persistent delays with more than 100,000 teachers unchecked
articles.summary: Three years after the government began mandatory vetting of teachers against the National Register for Sex Offenders, more than 119,000 educators across several provinces remain unchecked, raising serious concerns about learner safety and the pace of implementation.

articles.title: More than 1,300 migrants have died trying to reach the Spanish coast in 2026, says NGO
articles.summary: More than 1,300 migrants have died trying to reach the Spanish coast in the first five months of 2026, according to an advocacy group that tracks crossings from Africa along perilous routes through the Atlantic Ocean and western Mediterranean Sea

articles.title: POLITICALLY AWEH: Brett Herron pulls back the curtain on power, housing and politics
articles.summary: Back in 2018, the former Cape Town mayoral committee member resigned in protest at the DA’s approach to affordable housing. Eight years later, the crisis has only deepened, and will be a defining issue of the 2026 Local Government Elections. Now Herron is back, and he wants to be mayor.
